name: Almadén from Veredas-Brazatortas – circular by Roman ruins of Sisapo
name_original: Almadén desde Veredas-Brazatortas – circular por ruinas Romanas de Sisapo
region_intro: Road cycling around Brazatortas offers routes through the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, characterized by varied terrain including valleys and hills. The region features open landscapes and some historical points of interest. Road cyclists can expect a mix of moderate and challenging ascents across the area. Best road cycling routes around Brazatortas The most popular road cycling route is Castillo de Caracuel by Almodóvar del Campo — circular by Cabezarados, a 38.5 miles (61.9 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete, featuring a circular path. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Almodóvar del Campo y Argamasilla de Calatrava desde Brazatortas – circular por el Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, a moderate 42.8 miles (68.9 km) path. This route traverses the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona. Local road cyclists also love the Almodóvar del Campo and Abenó, a 62.3 miles (100.3 km) trail leading through open countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around Brazatortas is defined by routes through natural parks, valleys, and historical areas, offering options for different ability levels from moderate to challenging. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Brazatortas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Brazatortas?
A: Road cycling around Brazatortas offers a mix of moderate and challenging routes. The terrain includes varied landscapes with significant ascents, particularly as you traverse the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes around Brazatortas?
A: The routes featured in this guide are primarily moderate to difficult, with no easy routes listed. Cyclists should be prepared for varied terrain and ascents.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Brazatortas?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251056031/castillo-de-caracuel-by-almodovar-del-campo-circular-by-cabezarados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Caracuel by Almodóvar del Campo — circular by Cabezarados&lt;/a&gt;, or the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084926712/almodovar-del-campo-y-argamasilla-de-calatrava-desde-brazatortas-circular-por-el-parque-natural-del-valle-de-alcudia-y-sierra-madrona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar del Campo y Argamasilla de Calatrava desde Brazatortas&lt;/a&gt; route through the natural park.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes near Brazatortas?
A: The region is characterized by open landscapes, valleys, and hills, especially within the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona. You'll experience a mix of natural beauty and some historical points of interest along the way.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or attractions I can see while road cycling in Brazatortas?
A: While cycling, you might encounter natural wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lastras&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Batanera Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar Mill&lt;/a&gt; also add to the region's charm.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through natural parks?
A: Yes, several routes traverse the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084926712/almodovar-del-campo-y-argamasilla-de-calatrava-desde-brazatortas-circular-por-el-parque-natural-del-valle-de-alcudia-y-sierra-madrona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar del Campo y Argamasilla de Calatrava desde Brazatortas&lt;/a&gt; route, which offers extensive views of the park's diverse terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Brazatortas?
A: The region experiences hot summers, so spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for road cycling, offering milder temperatures and comfortable conditions for longer rides.

Q: Where can I typically start road cycling routes in the Brazatortas area?
A: Most road cycling routes in the region often start from or pass through towns like Brazatortas or Almodóvar del Campo. These locations usually offer convenient access and parking facilities.

Q: What kind of gear is essential for road cycling in Brazatortas?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for significant ascents, essential gear includes a well-maintained road bike, appropriate cycling apparel, a helmet, sufficient water, and navigation tools. Consider carrying spare tubes and a repair kit.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085143036/almaden-from-veredas-brazatortas-circular-by-roman-ruins-of-sisapo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén from Veredas-Brazatortas&lt;/a&gt; (128 km) or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070290629/sisapo-from-fuencaliente-circular-by-torrecampo-and-azuel-parque-natural-valle-de-alcudia-y-sierra-madrona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sisapo from Fuencaliente&lt;/a&gt; (128 km) offer substantial distances and considerable elevation gain, leading through open countryside and natural parks.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Brazatortas?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the region for its diverse terrain, which includes both valleys and hills, and the opportunity to cycle through natural parks like the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona. The blend of natural beauty and historical points of interest is also highly valued.
tips: An old mining town since Roman times, the town of Almadenejos is located on the border with the province of Córdoba, on the Almadén road in the Alcudia Valley.

World Heritage Site since 2012. Also called Plaza Nueva, whose construction was carried out between 1752 and 1754, it has a hexagonal shape and two stories high. It is made up of a single block, made up of twenty-four exterior homes, in whose interior courtyard the square itself was designed.

On the CM-4202 road, in the Alcudia Valley, you reach the La Bienvenida de Almodóvar del Campo site and the ruins of the ancient city of Sisapo. Roman settlement that exploited lead and silver from the region.
